tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute subprocesses (node CLI commands, npm run build, npm run tsc), run install-skills and check filesystem side effects (directory creation, file copying), and verify CLI help output. While some tests do read source files (test_route_capability_is_network, test_skill_file_exists), the majority test actual behavior via execution. The mix is acceptable as not 'every test' is pure text matching.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests require non-trivial implementation: test_install_skills_copies_files runs the CLI command in a temp directory and checks that actual files are created on disk including a references/ subdirectory. test_install_skills_command_exists requires the command to appear in help output. test_skill_file_exists requires a real file with specific content. A stub/empty implementation would not pass these tests.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution exists at /workspace/playwright and solve.sh is potentially accessible. The agent could read the solution patch or grep test files. The Dockerfile doesn't appear to remove /solution/ or test files. Also, some tests check for simple string presence (e.g., 'capability: \'network\'' in route.ts) which could be satisfied by appending a comment with that string rather than making the actual change.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All seven expected behaviors from the instruction are tested: install-skills command existence, file copying with references/, install-browser rename, route tools using network capability, network in config types, help text showing --in-memory (tested via test_in_memory_help_text which appears in the truncated tests), and SKILL.md existence/content.
